parsed.summary: The cumulative v0.4.0→v0.4.6 diff ships significant runtime-critical new code (retry, BigModel error dispatch, client rewrite) without end-to-end UAT evidence for v0.4.6, without unit-test coverage for the most-heavily-modified module (glm-client.mjs, +155 lines), and with a complete module deletion (process.mjs, 67 lines) whose import-cleanup completeness cannot be verified from the diff summary alone. Any one of these represents a plausible production failure path that is not gated by observable evidence.

findings.title: No v0.4.6 UAT evidence for retry/error-dispatch runtime paths
findings.body: Directories test-automation/uat-reports/v0.4.4/ and v0.4.5/ exist with full reports and run scripts. There is no test-automation/uat-reports/v0.4.6/ directory. The v0.4.6 release (d5fa754, e0a9ba8) introduced scripts/lib/bigmodel-errors.mjs (168 lines), scripts/lib/retry.mjs (203 lines), and significant glm-client.mjs modifications (+155 lines). These are the core request pipeline — error classification drives retry decisions which drive client behavior. The only validation evidence is unit tests (tests/retry.test.mjs, tests/bigmodel-errors.test.mjs) which cannot exercise the integrated retry→error-classify→client→state-mutation chain under real API failure conditions.
findings.recommendation: Add a v0.4.6 UAT run covering at minimum: (1) a BigModel API call that triggers each error category in bigmodel-errors.mjs, (2) retry exhaustion with backoff verification, and (3) a successful recovery after transient failure. Do not ship without UAT evidence for the new runtime paths.

findings.title: No unit tests for glm-client.mjs despite +155 lines of changes
findings.body: scripts/lib/glm-client.mjs received the largest modification in the core library (+155 lines). It integrates the new retry.mjs and bigmodel-errors.mjs modules. The test directory contains no glm-client.test.mjs. While tests/result-propagation.test.mjs (69 lines) may exercise some client paths indirectly, the client module now owns retry orchestration, error dispatch, and backoff — all logic that must be tested in isolation, especially around edge cases like partial responses, timeouts mid-stream, and concurrent request interleaving.
findings.recommendation: Create tests/glm-client.test.mjs covering: retry delegation on each BigModel error code, backoff parameter propagation, max-retry exhaustion, and the interaction between error classification and the caller's error path. This is the highest-delta module in the release and it has zero direct test coverage.

findings.title: process.mjs deleted (67 lines) — orphaned import risk
findings.body: scripts/lib/process.mjs was completely removed (67 lines deleted). The diff shows modifications to glm-companion.mjs, render.mjs, and other modules that presumably consumed process.mjs exports. However, with 84 files changed in the cumulative diff, the risk of a stale dynamic import, re-export barrel file reference, or lazy-loaded path that still points to process.mjs is non-trivial. A missed import would surface as a runtime ModuleNotFoundError only when the code path is exercised — exactly the class of bug UAT is meant to catch, and exactly the class where v0.4.6 UAT is absent.
findings.recommendation: Run `grep -r 'process\.mjs\|lib/process' scripts/ commands/ prompts/` against the working tree to confirm zero remaining references. Add a CI check (or extend scripts/ci/check-imports.mjs) that fails if any import target resolves to a nonexistent file.

next_steps: Create v0.4.6 UAT run covering retry, error dispatch, and client integration under simulated API failures.
Add tests/glm-client.test.mjs with direct unit coverage of the new retry/error-classification integration paths.
Grep the full tree for any remaining references to scripts/lib/process.mjs and add a CI gate that validates all import targets resolve.
Consider adding an integration smoke test that exercises the full companion→client→retry→state pipeline before tagging v0.4.7.
